description_long: This rifle features a classic design with a wooden fixed stock and foregrip, both finished in a dark brown color. The receiver, barrel, and magazine are constructed from black metal, giving the weapon a robust and utilitarian appearance. The rifle is equipped with a detachable box magazine and a prominent round drum, characteristic of early 20th-century submachine guns. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the barrel for aiming, and the muzzle is fitted with a simple cylindrical device. The overall build suggests a blend of wood and metal, with ergonomic grips and a sturdy frame. The weapon's proportions and details indicate it is designed for both functionality and durability in urban environments.
description: A classic rifle with a dark wooden stock and foregrip, black metal receiver, and detachable drum magazine, featuring iron sights and a cylindrical muzzle device.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the wooden stock, foregrip, and the drum magazine.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the circular profile of the drum magazine and the muzzle, with the barrel and iron sight visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first, with clear visibility of the wooden components and the magazine.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the back of the receiver, with the drum magazine partially visible.
